commit: # Load-invariant comparison via perf-stat instructions:u (deterministic to ~1k instr):
# Bottom line: Opt2 (DRBG key-sched cache) gives a real ~1% on sign-heavy workloads.
# Opt1 (288 in-place fp2 rewrites) saves ~0.3% on verify; LLVM was already SROA'ing most temps under LTO.
# Earlier "Rust 1.3-1.5x slower" claim was wrong (compared different workloads). Corrected:
# The 2x keygen/sign win is AES-NI (Rust `aes` crate auto-detect) vs C ref's bitsliced
# software AES (br_aes_ct64_* = 52% of C keygen profile). Verify (no DRBG/GMP) is at parity.

note: gf-fp2crate (verify-only; perf-stat: 6987M instr / 1603M cyc per 200 vfy; vs modarith 11018M / 2462M = 0.63x instr / 0.65x cyc; vs C-ref 49.3M-instr/vfy this is 34.9M = 0.71x)
